reviews.review_msg: Entertaining. Easier than other 360 profs in terms of expectations for assignments. Provided more than enough templates to make memos/letters easy.

Neil is a funny and very approachable prof. He brings in relevant personal stories to engage students interest.The in class assignments are based on interesting speakers and topics, such as Clinton and Reagan. It's a writing class, so keep up with the work. Ask anyone whose taken a class with him, he's one of the best Prof's SFU has to offer!

Neil is an interesting and approachable prof. Unfortunately, he seems to have little interest in the course as he frequently leaves class early after rushing through his powerpoints. I kept getting the feeling he couldn't wait to leave. His online class material is unorganized and contained errors. Luckily he gives out many A's.Textbook is useless.
One of the nicer SFU profs, he doesn't have a condescending attitude.  Instead, he is helpful, straightforward, and very supportive of students.  Great prof!
Great instructor with a good attitude.  Good sense of humour and great powerpoints and videos.
Fabulous instructor - funny, approachable, and kind.  His lectures are from the heart.

He is nice, cheerful and willing to help if you are willing to ask.  He transformed the whole notion that BUS 360 was a hard course. Must admit that his lectures were sometimes irrelevant to the textbook but then, he highlighted all the main points well enough that you'll have suffiicent writing skills for the business world.

Transforms what would be a useful but boring topic into a completely boring and useless class. Lectures are a waste of time. I'd rather be eating poo. STOP READING OFF OF YOUR NOTES AND INTERACT WITH US. My god. Take it with Kevin Stewart. He may be more difficult, but at least you don't feel like you just wasted $800.
Energetic, clear, funny, insightful, and very passionate about what he does.  Love the personal stories and self disclosure.  He should teach more courses.  Pretty hot too.
